findings.evidence_context: const buyerA = "Buyer.A@Example.com" // mixed-case on purpose: readers must lowercase + trim. RFC 2606 reserved @example.com domain; file header states 'All emails use the RFC 2606 example.com domain so they can never resolve to a real mailbox.'
const buyerB = "buyer.b@example.com" — second synthetic fixture constant alongside buyerA. RFC 2606 reserved @example.com domain, used only to seed the temp SQLite store in unit tests.
Assertion in TestReadEBOrders_DedupAndNullSafety: 'got.Email != "buyer.a@example.com"' — the lowercased form of the buyerA fixture, asserting the reader normalizes case. RFC 2606 reserved domain.
Assertion in TestReadEBAttendees_CheckinAndNameFallback: 'got.Email != "buyer.a@example.com"' — lowercased buyerA fixture, asserting attendee-email normalization. RFC 2606 reserved domain.
